options.name: boot.kernelPackages
options.description: This option allows you to override the Linux kernel used by NixOS. Since things like external kernel module packages are tied to the kernel you're using, it also overrides those. …

options.name: boot.kernelParams
options.type: list of string, with spaces inside double quotes
options.description: Parameters added to the kernel command line.

options.name: boot.loader.efi.canTouchEfiVariables
options.description: Whether the installation process is allowed to modify EFI boot variables.

options.name: boot.loader.grub.device
options.description: The device on which the GRUB boot loader will be installed. The special value `nodev` means that a GRUB boot menu will be generated, but GRUB itself will not actually be installed. To install GRUB on multiple devices, use `boot.loader.grub.devices`.

options.name: boot.loader.grub.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the GNU GRUB boot loader.

options.name: boot.loader.systemd-boot.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the systemd-boot (formerly gummiboot) EFI boot manager. For more information about systemd-boot: https://www.freedesktop.org/wiki/Software/systemd/systemd-boot/

options.name: boot.tmp.cleanOnBoot
options.description: Whether to delete all files in `/tmp` during boot.

options.name: console.keyMap
options.description: The keyboard mapping table for the virtual consoles.

options.name: documentation.enable
options.description: Whether to install documentation of packages from `environment.systemPackages` into the generated system path. See "Multiple-output packages" chapter in the nixpkgs manual for more info.

options.name: environment.systemPackages
options.description: The set of packages that appear in /run/current-system/sw. These packages are automatically available to all users, and are automatically updated every time you rebuild the system configuration. …

options.name: environment.variables
options.type: attribute set of ((list of (floating point number or signed integer or string)) or floating point number or signed integer or string or path)
options.description: A set of environment variables used in the global environment. These variables will be set on shell initialisation (e.g. in /etc/profile). The value of each variable can be either a string or a list of strings. The latter is concatenated, interspersed with colon characters.

options.name: fonts.packages
options.description: List of primary font packages.

options.name: hardware.bluetooth.enable
options.description: Whether to enable support for Bluetooth.

options.name: hardware.opengl.enable
options.description: Whether to enable OpenGL drivers. This is needed to enable OpenGL support in X11 systems, as well as for Wayland compositors like sway and Weston. …

options.name: hardware.pulseaudio.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the PulseAudio sound server.

options.name: i18n.defaultLocale
options.description: The default locale. It determines the language for program messages, the format for dates and times, sort order, and so on. It also determines the character set, such as UTF-8.

options.name: networking.enableIPv6
options.description: Whether to enable support for IPv6.

options.name: networking.firewall.allowedTCPPortRanges
options.type: list of attribute set of 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)
options.description: A range of TCP ports on which incoming connections are accepted.

options.name: networking.firewall.allowedTCPPorts
options.type: list of 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)
options.description: List of TCP ports on which incoming connections are accepted.

options.name: networking.firewall.allowedUDPPorts
options.type: list of 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)
options.description: List of open UDP ports.

options.name: networking.firewall.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the firewall. This is a simple stateful firewall that blocks connection attempts to unauthorised TCP or UDP ports on this machine.

options.name: networking.hostName
options.description: The name of the machine. Leave it empty if you want to obtain it from a DHCP server (if using DHCP). The hostname must be a valid DNS label (see RFC 1035 section 2.3.1: "Preferred name syntax", RFC 1123 section 2.1: "Host Names and Numbers") and as such must not contain the domain part. …

options.name: networking.nameservers
options.description: The list of nameservers. It can be left empty if it is auto-detected through DHCP.

options.name: networking.networkmanager.enable
options.description: Whether to use NetworkManager to obtain an IP address and other configuration for all network interfaces that are not manually configured. If enabled, a group `networkmanager` will be created. Add all users that should have permission to change network settings to this group.

options.name: networking.useDHCP
options.description: Whether to use DHCP to obtain an IP address and other configuration for all network interfaces that do not have any manually configured IPv4 addresses.

options.name: nix.gc.automatic
options.description: Automatically run the garbage collector at a specific time.

options.name: powerManagement.enable
options.description: Whether to enable power management. This includes support for suspend-to-RAM and powersave features on laptops.

options.name: programs.fish.enable
options.description: Whether to configure fish as an interactive shell.

options.name: programs.git.enable
options.description: Whether to enable git, a distributed version control system.

options.name: programs.gnupg.agent.enable
options.description: Enables GnuPG agent with socket-activation for every user session.

options.name: programs.vim.defaultEditor
options.description: When enabled, installs vim and configures vim to be the default editor using the EDITOR environment variable.

options.name: programs.zsh.enable
options.description: Whether to configure zsh as an interactive shell. To enable zsh for a particular user, use the `users.users.<name?>.shell` option for that user. To enable zsh system-wide use the `users.defaultUserShell` option.

options.name: security.sudo.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the `sudo` command, which allows non-root users to execute commands as root.

options.name: security.sudo.wheelNeedsPassword
options.description: Whether users of the `wheel` group must provide a password to run commands as super user via `sudo`.

options.name: services.avahi.enable
options.description: Whether to run the Avahi daemon, which allows Avahi clients to use Avahi's service discovery facilities and also allows the local machine to advertise its presence and services (through the mDNS responder implemented by `avahi-daemon`).

options.name: services.dbus.enable
options.description: Whether to start the D-Bus message bus daemon, which is required by many other system services and applications.

options.name: services.fail2ban.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the fail2ban service. See the documentation of `services.fail2ban.jails` for what jails are enabled by default.

options.name: services.getty.autologinUser
options.description: Username of the account that will be automatically logged in at the console. If unspecified, a login prompt is shown as usual.

options.name: services.nginx.enable
options.description: Whether to enable Nginx Web Server.

options.name: services.ntp.enable
options.description: Whether to synchronise your machine's time using ntpd, as a peer in the NTP network. Disables `systemd.timesyncd` if enabled.

options.name: services.openssh.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the OpenSSH secure shell daemon, which allows secure remote logins.

options.name: services.openssh.openFirewall
options.description: Whether to automatically open the specified ports in the firewall.

options.name: services.openssh.ports
options.type: list of 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)
options.description: Specifies on which ports the SSH daemon listens.

options.name: services.pipewire.enable
options.description: Whether to enable PipeWire service.

options.name: services.postgresql.enable
options.description: Whether to enable PostgreSQL Server.

options.name: services.postgresql.package
options.description: The postgresql package to use.

options.name: services.printing.enable
options.description: Whether to enable printing support through the CUPS daemon.

options.name: services.resolved.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the systemd DNS resolver daemon, `systemd-resolved`. Search for `services.resolved` to see all options.

options.name: services.tailscale.enable
options.description: Whether to enable Tailscale client daemon.

options.name: services.xserver.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the X server.

options.name: sound.enable
options.description: Whether to enable ALSA sound.

options.name: system.stateVersion
options.description: This option defines the first version of NixOS you have installed on this particular machine, and is used to maintain compatibility with application data (e.g. databases) created on older NixOS versions. …

options.name: time.timeZone
options.description: The time zone used when displaying times and dates. See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones> for a comprehensive list of possible values for this setting. If null, the timezone will default to UTC and can be set imperatively using timedatectl.

options.name: users.groups
options.description: Additional groups to be created automatically by the system.

options.name: users.mutableUsers
options.description: If set to `true`, you are free to add new users and groups to the system with the ordinary `useradd` and `groupadd` commands. …

options.name: users.users
options.description: Additional user accounts to be created automatically by the system. This can also be used to set options for root.

options.name: virtualisation.docker.enable
options.description: This option enables docker, a daemon that manages linux containers. Users in the "docker" group can interact with the daemon (e.g. to start or stop containers) using the `docker` command line tool.

options.name: virtualisation.libvirtd.enable
options.description: This option enables libvirtd, a daemon that manages virtual machines. Users in the "libvirtd" group can interact with the daemon (e.g. to start or stop VMs) using the `virsh` command line tool, among others.

options.name: zramSwap.enable
options.description: Enable in-memory compressed devices and swap space provided by the zram kernel module. See [ https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/blockdev/zram.txt ](https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/blockdev/zram.txt).
